title: sida malaccensis
intro: Sida malaccensis, also known as the Malayan sida or sida cordifolia, is a plant species in the mallow family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Sida malaccensis include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and astringent. Sida malaccensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
